sections.paragraph:
θάρσος, ους, τό (Hom. et al.; Epict. 1, 24, 8; 2, 13, 3; SIG 709, 25; PCairMasp 158, 16; LXX, Philo, Joseph.; SibOr 1, 241) courage θ. λαμβάνειν take courage (Jos., Ant. 9, 55) Ac 28:15 (for assoc. w. deity cp. Plut., Mor. 34a); Hv 3, 1, 5. W. χαρά MPol 12:1.—Schmidt, Syn. III 543–50, cp. ἀνδρεῖος. TW. Spicq.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

PCairMasp PCairMasp = JMaspero, Papyrus grecs d’époque byzantine—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Hv Hv = Visions

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

Schmidt, Syn. Schmidt, Syn. = JS., Synonymik—List 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 444
